title: Support missing second type in for-loop variable definition
body:
It looks like support for multiple variable definitions in a for-loop initializer is incorrect (introduced with d4bb58630efcdfdcfae09bd691561720173a8ffc). I'm trying to use the following syntax (example from SystemVerilog specification 1800-2023 § 12.7.1 The for-loop):
``` verilog
for ( int count = 0, done = 0, j = 0; j * count < 125; j++, count++) $display("Value j = %d\n", j );
```

Verilator appears to accept the following illegal statement: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/0fed5f8b3e02088ee22df4c8b27e6e5e386ce227/test_regress/t/t_for_comma.v#L51

I verified this issue exists in the latest `master` (0fed5f8b3e02088ee22df4c8b27e6e5e386ce227).

```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.023 devel rev v5.020-152-g0fed5f8b3

$ sw_vers
ProductName:            macOS
ProductVersion:         14.2.1
BuildVersion:           23C71

$ cat test.sv
module t;
initial for (int a = 0, b = 0; ;) ;
endmodule

$ verilator --lint-only test.sv
%Error: test.sv:2:25: Can't find definition of variable: 'b'
    2 | initial for (int a = 0, b = 0; ;) ;
      |                         ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```
